title: PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellant) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent) (formerly VV (Jordan) (FC) and PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellants) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent))

metadata.title: PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellant) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent) (formerly VV (Jordan) (FC) and PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellants) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent))
metadata.summary: PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellant) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent) (formerly VV (Jordan) (FC) and PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellants) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent))
extracts: PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellant) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent) (formerly VV (Jordan) (FC) and PP (Algeria) (FC) (Appellants) v Secretary of State for the Home Department (Respondent))

1. From time to time over many years the Secretary of State for the Home Department has been concerned to deport a foreign national on the grounds of national security. Sometimes, indeed with increasing frequency, those facing such deportation decisions have wished to contest them, either by challenging that they present a national security risk, or by invoking the European Convention on Human Rights and contending that they would be at risk of article 3 ill-treatment if returned to their home country.
metadata.title_key: pp v secretary of state for the home department and pp v secretary of state for the home department
